mechanism_narrative: A persistent downward trend in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and 20-day forward SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive returns following FEDFUNDS declines occur at a rate statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional base rate (65.5%).

mechanism_narrative: A Sahm-rule-like breach where UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points above its trailing 12-month low signals the onset of a labor-market-driven recession, triggering risk-off equity flows and negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.3% in latest vintage, near multi-year lows
failure_condition: Following triggered breaches, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the average post-trigger return is not significantly below the unconditional mean at p<0.05 across the 2005-2024 sample.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) reflects synchronized consumer and residential investment strength that is not captured by either series alone; this combined demand impulse predicts above-average S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because it signals broad-based economic momentum feeding corporate earnings.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST 3-month annualized growth exceed their respective 5-year median simultaneously
failure_condition: The joint-trigger subsample shows forward 20-day return distribution statistically indistinguishable (KS test p>0.10) from the subsample where only one or neither series is accelerating, indicating no incremental interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, deceleration in INDPRO growth below its trailing 12-month average signals waning industrial capacity utilization and impending margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth falls below its trailing 12-month average
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns following INDPRO deceleration in late-cycle windows are not significantly lower than in early/mid-cycle windows (t-test p>0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL-based inflation (i.e., real policy rate turns strongly positive above 2%), tight financial conditions restrict credit availability and compress equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month until the real rate normalizes.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation rate exceeds 2.0 percentage points
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive real rates
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward SPX return during triggered high-real-rate periods is not significantly below the unconditional sample mean (p>0.05), or triggered periods show positive returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) reflects continued labor market strength that supports consumer spending and near-term corporate revenue, producing modestly positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week regardless of broader regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and 5-day forward SPX return remains below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating no persistent short-horizon predictive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a sustained rebound in HOUST (housing starts) above its trailing 6-month average signals renewed residential investment and consumer confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as the recovery broadens into other sectors.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its trailing 6-month average for two consecutive months
failure_condition: During post-recession recovery windows, triggered HOUST rebounds are not followed by above-median 60-day SPX returns relative to non-triggered periods (Mann-Whitney p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of rising UNRATE with a simultaneously high FEDFUNDS level indicates the central bank is maintaining restrictive policy despite labor market deterioration, a combination historically preceding sharp equity drawdowns because policy easing is delayed relative to economic need, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases > 0.2pp over 3 months while FEDFUNDS remains above 3.5%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.63% and UNRATE at 4.3% in latest vintage
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle amid labor market softening
failure_condition: Joint-trigger subsample forward 60-day returns are not significantly more negative than the subsample where only UNRATE rises without elevated FEDFUNDS (t-test p>0.05), indicating no interaction effect beyond the single-series labor signal.
